answers: Plastic pollution occurs when plastic waste accumulates in the environment, harming wildlife, ecosystems, and human health. Over 8 million tons of plastic enter the oceans every year.
Plastic pollution holo plastic waste environment e joma howa. Plastic decompose hote 500+ bochor lage, ar eta ocean, soil, ar air sob pollute kore.
Microplastics — tiny plastic particles less than 5mm — are now found everywhere: in oceans, drinking water, food, and even human blood. They come from degrading larger plastics and synthetic fabrics.
To fight plastic pollution, reduce single-use plastics, support bans on plastic bags, choose reusable products, recycle properly, and support organizations working on ocean cleanup.
